LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FORD MOTOR COMPANY AND PALM SPRINGS MOTORS, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BARNES CONTENDS THAT HIS CHOICE OF VENUE IS PROPER SOLELY BECAUSE HE HAS SUED A FOREIGN CORPORATION WHO HAS NOT DESIGNATED A PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA. (SEE EASTON V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243.) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT NONE OF THE UNDERLYING ACTIONS GIVING RISE TO HIS CLAIMS TOOK PLACE IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. IT IS TRUE THAT IF A CORPORATE DEFENDANT FAILS TO FILE A STATEMENT DESIGNATING A PRINCIPAL OFFICE IN CALIFORNIA, VENUE IS PROPER IN ANY COUNTY IN CALIFORNIA. NEVERTHELESS, "[I]T IS ONLY WHEN NONE OF THE DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN THE STATE THAT THE ACTION MAY BE TRIED IN ANY COUNTY WHICH THE PLAINTIFF MAY DESIGNATE IN HIS COMPLAINT." (BANTA V. WINK, (1897) 119 CAL. 78, 80; SEE ALSO LUDINGTON V. LA FORTUNA GOLD, (1906) 4 CAL.APP. 369, 370.) IN THIS CASE, PALM SPRINGS MOTORS RESIDES IN RIVERSIDE COUNTY. THEREFORE, THERE IS NO BASIS FOR VENUE IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR FEES PURSUANT TO CCP 396B(B) AND CCP 128.5(A) IS DENIED. MS. CHENG IS PROVIDE THE PROPOSED ORDER TO MS. LEE FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM. JUDGE: JOHN STEWART. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Defendants Ford Motor Company And Palm Springs Motors, Inc'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Lee of Strategic Legal Practices, APC telephone number 310-929-4900 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff William Barnes.
Jamie Cheng of LeClair Ryan LLP telephone number 415-913-4925 for Defendants Ford Motor Company and Palm Springs Motors, Inc.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Ford Motor Company And Palm Springs Motors, Inc's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is taken off calendar pursuant to the court's ruling on Defendants Ford Motor Company and Palm Springs Motors, Inc.' motion to transfer venue. (See Pickwick Stages System v. Superior Court (1934) 138 Cal.App. 448.)

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Defendants Palm Springs Motors, Inc. And Ford Motor Company'S Motion For Change Of Venue To Riverside County
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Lee of Strategic Legal Practices, APC telephone number 310-929-4900 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff William Barnes.
Jamie Cheng of LeClair Ryan LLP telephone number 415-913-4925 for Defendants Ford Motor Company and Palm Springs Motors, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Ford Motor Company and Palm Springs Motors, Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Riverside County is granted. Plaintiff William Barnes contends that his choice of venue is proper solely because he has sued a foreign corporation who has not designated a principal place of business in California. (See Easton v. Superior Court, (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243.) Plaintiff does not dispute that none of the underlying actions giving rise to his claims took place in San Francisco County. It is true that if a corporate defendant fails to file a statement designating a principal office in California, venue is proper in any county in California. Nevertheless, "[i]t is only when none of the defendants reside in the state that the action may be tried in any county which the plaintiff may designate in his complaint." (Banta v. Wink, (1897) 119 Cal. 78, 80; see also Ludington v. La Fortuna Gold, (1906) 4 Cal.App. 369, 370.) In this case, Palm Springs Motors resides in Riverside County. Therefore, there is no basis for venue in San Francisco County. Defendants' request for fees pursuant to CCP 396b(b) and CCP 128.5(a) is denied. Ms. Cheng is provide the proposed order to Ms. Lee for approval as to form.

Judge: John Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
